# Kleros Oracle

> Kleros is the resolution layer for prediction markets: an optimistic oracle
> (Reality.eth) backstopped by Kleros Court, an on-chain jury arbitration system.

Live since 2018. Resolving prediction-market disputes since 2020 (Omen, now
Presagio, was first to integrate). 2,653 disputes resolved on-chain across
Ethereum, Gnosis and Arbitrum. 750+ jurors participating.

## Key facts

- Mechanism: a question is posted on Reality.eth with a bond, naming Kleros as
  arbitrator. Anyone answers or challenges by doubling the bond; most questions
  settle there. Genuine disagreements escalate to Kleros Court, where jurors are
  drawn randomly, weighted by staked PNK, from a topic-relevant subcourt.
- Appeals double the jury each round (3 → 7 → 15 → 31 → …), making it
  progressively more expensive to overturn a well-evidenced answer.
- Jurors cannot choose their case and cannot be chosen: stake raises the
  probability of being drawn but never buys the seat. 25% of stake is a 25%
  chance of being drawn, not 25% of the verdict.
- Versus UMA: Kleros uses a fixed-size per-case jury from a topic-relevant
  subcourt with evidence and appeals; UMA uses a token-weighted vote of the
  entire holder base with escalation to the DVM. Kleros cases mostly resolve in
  a day; heavily appealed cases can take a couple of weeks.
- Integrations: Seer, Omen/Presagio, Foresight, Precog, Futarchy.fi,
  Deep Funding, Ringo.
- Available on Ethereum, Arbitrum, Gnosis, Polygon, Base, Optimism, Unichain;
  Solana integration in progress.

## Full content
